lens: Fresh multi-bank deal flow keeps landing even as the tape rolls over (Nasdaq -1.0%, S&P -0.4%): Eli Lilly's ~$2.8B buy of AtaiBeckley (Goldman buy-side; Moelis, Centerview and Citi sell-side), Big Brand's acquisition of Belle Tire (Evercore, Jefferies), Wonder's $650M Series D (Goldman, Jefferies, JPMorgan placement), and First Hawaiian's now-definitive $1.0B+ acquisition of TriCo (Evercore; KBW/Stifel).

stories.headline: Goldman advises Eli Lilly on its ~$2.8B acquisition of AtaiBeckley (up to ~$3.8B with CVRs)
stories.why_it_matters: Eli Lilly signed a definitive agreement to acquire AtaiBeckley for ~$2.8B upfront ($6.75/share) plus up to ~$1.0B in milestone CVRs ($2.50/share), advancing therapies for treatment-resistant depression (candidates BPL-003, VLS-01). The release names Goldman Sachs as exclusive financial adviser to Lilly (the buyer). A large-cap biopharma acquisition with heavy scientific, regulatory, and CVR-structuring diligence.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Goldman's healthcare/biopharma coverage on the Lilly/AtaiBeckley signing — a fresh large-cap CNS acquisition with a CVR structure is a document- and diligence-heavy use case.

deal_clock.event: Lilly signs definitive agreement to acquire AtaiBeckley

stories.headline: Moelis and Centerview advise AtaiBeckley on its ~$2.8B sale to Eli Lilly (Citi also advises the board)
stories.why_it_matters: On the sell-side of Lilly's AtaiBeckley acquisition, the release names Moelis & Company and Centerview Partners as financial advisers to AtaiBeckley, with Citi also providing financial advice to the AtaiBeckley board. A multi-adviser large-cap biopharma sell-side with CVR consideration and cross-border (UK/US) diligence.
stories.suggested_action: Engage Moelis's healthcare team on the AtaiBeckley sale — a multi-adviser biopharma sell-side spins up a controlled, multi-party diligence process immediately.

deal_clock.event: AtaiBeckley agrees to ~$2.8B sale to Lilly

stories.headline: Evercore advises Big Brand Tire on its acquisition of Belle Tire (Jefferies advises Belle Tire)
stories.why_it_matters: Big Brand Tire & Service signed a definitive agreement to acquire Belle Tire, creating a tire-and-auto-service platform with >$1.5B combined revenue, expected to close Q3 2026. The release names Evercore as exclusive financial adviser to Big Brand (buyer) and Jefferies as exclusive financial adviser to Belle Tire (seller). A sponsor-backed consumer/services consolidation with two-sided diligence.
stories.suggested_action: Talk to Evercore's consumer/services coverage on the Belle Tire acquisition — a signed platform combination heading to a Q3 close is a live diligence and integration use case.

deal_clock.event: Big Brand Tire signs deal to acquire Belle Tire

stories.headline: Jefferies advises Belle Tire on its sale to Big Brand Tire & Service
stories.why_it_matters: On the sell-side of the Big Brand/Belle Tire combination, the release names Jefferies as exclusive financial adviser to Belle Tire. A consumer/automotive-services sell-side to a strategic buyer, expected to close in Q3 2026 with active diligence now.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Jefferies's sponsor/consumer coverage on the Belle Tire sale — a signed strategic sale is a concrete near-term diligence conversation.

deal_clock.event: Belle Tire agrees to sale to Big Brand Tire

stories.headline: Goldman, Jefferies and JPMorgan are placement agents on Wonder's $650M Series D ($9B pre-money)
stories.why_it_matters: Food-tech company Wonder raised a $650M Series D at a $9B pre-money valuation, with new and existing investors (AllianceBernstein, ARK Invest, Kayne Anderson Rudnick, Accel, GV, NEA). The release names Goldman Sachs, Jefferies and J.P. Morgan as placement agents. A large late-stage private raise with investor-diligence and data-room workstreams.
stories.suggested_action: Use the Wonder raise as a private-capital-markets hook with JPMorgan's (and Goldman's/Jefferies') placement teams — large late-stage rounds run controlled investor diligence that maps directly to secure data rooms.

deal_clock.event: Wonder announces $650M Series D at $9B pre-money

stories.headline: First Hawaiian/TriCo merger goes definitive; Evercore advises FHB, KBW (a Stifel company) advises TriCo
stories.why_it_matters: First Hawaiian (NASDAQ: FHB) and TriCo Bancshares (NASDAQ: TCBK) signed a definitive agreement for First Hawaiian to acquire TriCo in an all-stock deal creating a leading Pacific banking franchise; the merger agreement (EX-2.1) was filed Jul 15. The press release names Evercore as financial adviser to First Hawaiian and Keefe, Bruyette & Woods (A Stifel Company) as financial adviser to TriCo. A signed public bank merger now advancing to the proxy/vote phase.
stories.suggested_action: Reach out to Evercore's FIG team on the now-definitive TriCo deal — a signed bank merger triggers two-sided diligence and integration planning heading into the shareholder vote.
stories.source: SEC EDGAR — TriCo EX-99.1 (press release) and EX-2.1 (merger agreement)

stories.headline: Stocks slip as tech leads lower: Nasdaq -1.0%, S&P -0.4%, Dow holds +0.2%
stories.why_it_matters: US equities opened softer on Jul 16, with the Nasdaq off 1.0% (26,003) and the S&P 500 down 0.4% (7,545), while the Dow held modestly positive (+0.2%, 52,754). The VIX ticked up to ~16.3 and the 10Y yield firmed to ~4.59%. Bank stocks pulled back after the earnings run (GS -2.2%, MS -2.2%, JPM -0.9%). A pause in the tape doesn't slow signed deal flow — diligence timelines run regardless of the day's index prints.
stories.suggested_action: Frame outreach around deal-process continuity: signed mandates (Lilly/AtaiBeckley, Belle Tire, TriCo) keep diligence clocks running even on a red day — a natural reason to talk room readiness now.

opportunities.headline: Adviser to First Hawaiian on its all-stock acquisition of TriCo Bancshares
opportunities.why_it_matters: Evercore advises First Hawaiian on its all-stock acquisition of TriCo Bancshares — now under a definitive merger agreement filed Jul 15 (KBW/Stifel advises TriCo). A signed public bank merger triggers two-sided diligence and integration planning into the shareholder vote.
opportunities.outreach_idea: Open on the now-definitive First Hawaiian/TriCo agreement — a signed bank merger is a concrete reason to engage Evercore's FIG team on diligence infrastructure.

outreach_draft.subject: First Hawaiian / TriCo — supporting a signed bank merger
outreach_draft.body:
Congratulations on Evercore's role advising First Hawaiian on the TriCo Bancshares combination, now under a definitive agreement. All-stock bank mergers spin up two-sided diligence and integration workstreams heading into the vote — would welcome a conversation on how we support clean, auditable data-room access.

opportunities.headline: Adviser to Apogee on AbbVie's $135.11/share cash acquisition
opportunities.why_it_matters: Goldman (with Jefferies) advises Apogee Therapeutics on AbbVie's $135.11/sh all-cash take-private. Definitive proxy filed Jul 13 — the vote and diligence phase is live in a large-cap biopharma deal.
opportunities.outreach_idea: Open on Apogee/AbbVie — a filed large-cap biopharma take-private is a concrete reason to engage Goldman's healthcare team.

outreach_draft.subject: Apogee / AbbVie — supporting a large-cap take-private
outreach_draft.body:
Congratulations on Goldman's role advising Apogee Therapeutics on the AbbVie acquisition. All-cash biopharma take-privates run heavy, time-sensitive diligence through the shareholder vote — would welcome a conversation on how we support secure, auditable data-room access to close.

opportunities.headline: Adviser on the AvalonBay / Equity Residential all-stock REIT merger
opportunities.why_it_matters: Goldman advises on the AvalonBay/Equity Residential combination (2.793 EQR per AVB share) — one of the largest US apartment REIT mergers. Joint proxy filed Jul 13; integration diligence is live.
opportunities.outreach_idea: Engage Goldman's real estate coverage on the AvalonBay/Equity Residential merger — a mega-cap REIT combination with a fresh proxy.

outreach_draft.subject: AvalonBay / Equity Residential — supporting a mega-cap REIT merger
outreach_draft.body:

opportunities.headline: Adviser to IHS Holding board on MTN's $8.50/share cash take-private
opportunities.why_it_matters: JPMorgan advises the IHS Holding board on MTN Group's $8.50/sh cash take-private, funded partly by a $1.0B term loan. Tender/merger materials filed Jul 10 — a document-heavy, cross-border process in motion.
opportunities.outreach_idea: Engage JPMorgan's telecom-infrastructure coverage on IHS — a cross-border cash take-private with financing is a strong diligence use case.

outreach_draft.subject: IHS Holding — supporting a cross-border take-private
outreach_draft.body:

market_snapshot.macro_note: Tape softened Jul 16 (S&P -0.4%, Nasdaq -1.0%; Dow held +0.2%). VIX ~16.3, 10Y ~4.59%. Bank stocks pulled back post-earnings (GS/MS ~-2.2%, JPM -0.9%). Data live from the Jul 16 open session; BAC, C, LAZ, JEF, HLI and EVR omitted where only stale Jul 15 quotes were verifiable — accuracy over completeness.

talking_point.quote: Even on a red tape, the deals keep signing — Lilly’s $2.8B AtaiBeckley buy, Big Brand/Belle Tire, First Hawaiian/TriCo going definitive, Wonder’s $650M round. Signed mandates run their diligence clocks regardless of the day’s index prints. How are you thinking about room readiness across the processes you’re launching this quarter?
talking_point.use_with: M&A and advisory coverage teams (esp. Goldman, Moelis, Evercore, Jefferies, JPMorgan, Citi)
talking_point.context: Anchors on a busy Jul 16 deal tape against a down market — a natural way to separate deal-process momentum from the day’s volatility and open a room-readiness conversation.

ranked.read: Lilly’s ~$2.8B AtaiBeckley buy (Goldman buy-side; Moelis, Centerview, Citi sell-side) headlines fresh large-cap CNS M&A, on top of AbbVie/Apogee.
First Hawaiian/TriCo goes definitive (Evercore; KBW/Stifel) — a Pacific bank-merger of scale now heading to the vote.
Big Brand acquires Belle Tire (Evercore, Jefferies) — a >$1.5B-revenue platform combination.
Wonder’s $650M Series D at $9B pre-money (Goldman, Jefferies, JPMorgan placement) signals live late-stage private-raise diligence.

pipeline_watch.note: Named covered-bank mandates and sector heat. Rumored / no-adviser situations live in the Rumor Mill.

skeptics_corner.note: Unconfirmed leads — rumored deals and situations with no adviser named yet (or advisers unconfirmed). Real reporting, but treat as speculative: chase them, don’t quote them on a call.

items.lead: Auto-insurance software firm CCC is exploring a sale and has reached out to prospective buyers including PE firms, with activist Elliott holding a large stake; Morgan Stanley reported to be advising.
items.why_unverified: Reuters/Bloomberg report citing people familiar; CCC and Morgan Stanley declined/did not comment, no deal announced.

items.lead: Diagnostics maker Qiagen has drawn early takeover interest from EQT, Advent and KKR, with suitors indicating offers of at least $50/share as part of a strategic review begun in January.
items.why_unverified: Bloomberg report citing people familiar; discussions preliminary, no formal offer, advisers not confirmed.

items.lead: Stripe and PE firm Advent International have made a ~$53B ($60.50/share) unsolicited takeover approach for PayPal, backed by ~$50B in committed bank financing.
items.why_unverified: Reuters report citing sources; PayPal has left the bid unanswered, no adviser named, no certainty it leads to a deal.

items.lead: Lionsgate Studios is exploring a sale and has attracted interest from Bollore Group, Banijay and Mediawan, reportedly working with an unnamed investment bank to assess suitors.
items.why_unverified: Reuters report citing sources; representatives declined to comment, bank unnamed, a deal is not certain.

items.lead: Activist Randian Capital sent a letter urging loanDepot’s board to court acquirers, arguing its $120B+ servicing portfolio could command a premium in a strategic transaction.
items.why_unverified: Activist letter only; company declined to comment, no adviser or process, founder holds controlling position.

items.lead: Harwood Capital’s Christopher Mills (8%+ holder) is publicly pushing for a breakup/piecemeal sale of ad group M&C Saatchi, predicting it won’t be independent within a year.
items.why_unverified: Activist commentary only; company declined to comment, no formal process or adviser mandate.
